title: Values, Expressions, Names, and Types · Changed-Input Lab
summary: Build a transparent unit and budget calculator whose intermediate values can be inspected and tested. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an inspectable unit and budget calculator with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish values, objects, names, assignment, equality, and identity in Python execution.
Use integers, floating-point values, decimal-sensitive decisions, arithmetic precedence, and division operators accurately.
Use Boolean values and None as distinct domain states instead of ambiguous numeric or text sentinels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-basics cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.
